summary: Completed frontend testing of the auto-start timer modal feature after creating a new OT (Ordem de Trabalho). All 9 test cases passed successfully.

passed_tests.test: Modal 'Iniciar Cronómetro' appears automatically after creating OT
passed_tests.evidence: Modal appeared with title 'Iniciar Cronómetro - OT #362'
OT numbers #362, #363, #364 displayed correctly in modal title

passed_tests.test: Technician list loads correctly with names
passed_tests.evidence: List shows: Pedro Duarte, Miguel Moreira, Gichelson Leite, Nuno Santos
Buttons toggle correctly - Trabalho (blue) and Viagem (orange) highlight when selected
Successfully selected Pedro Duarte and Miguel Moreira with checkmarks
Counter showed '1 técnico(s) selecionado(s)' and '2 técnico(s) selecionado(s)'

passed_tests.test: Ignorar button closes modal without starting timer
passed_tests.evidence: Modal closed, no timer started (verified in backend logs)

passed_tests.test: Iniciar button starts timers for selected technicians
passed_tests.evidence: Backend logs show: 'Cronómetro viagem iniciado para Pedro Duarte' and 'Cronómetro viagem iniciado para Miguel Moreira'

passed_tests.test: Success toast appears after starting timer
passed_tests.evidence: Toast message: 'Cronómetro de Viagem iniciado para 2 técnico(s)!'

context_for_next_testing_agent: The auto-start timer modal feature is fully functional. Test flow: Login -> /technical-reports -> Click 'Ver' on client -> Click 'Adicionar Relatório' -> Fill form (Local, Pedido por, Motivo) -> Click 'Criar OT' -> Modal appears automatically. The modal allows selecting timer type (Trabalho/Viagem) and multiple technicians. Backend endpoint /api/relatorios-tecnicos/{id}/cronometro/iniciar is working correctly.
